evals.prompt: Refactor UserDto.java to follow our Lombok-first conventions. The class has hand-written getters, setters, a constructor, toString(), and equals/hashCode that should all be replaced with Lombok annotations. Lombok is on the classpath. Output the refactored UserDto.java.
evals.expected_output: UserDto using @Data (or equivalent @Getter/@Setter/@ToString/@EqualsAndHashCode/@RequiredArgsConstructor combination) with no manual getters, setters, toString, equals, or hashCode.

assertions.text: No hand-written getter or setter methods
Uses Lombok annotations for data-class boilerplate
Does not import lombok.val or lombok.var

evals.prompt: Refactor GreetingService.java to follow our Lombok-first conventions. The class has a hand-written @Inject constructor and a manual SLF4J Logger field. Replace them with the appropriate Lombok annotations. Lombok is on the classpath.
evals.expected_output: GreetingService with @RequiredArgsConstructor(onConstructor_ = @Inject) and @Slf4j, no manual constructor body, no manual Logger field.

assertions.text: Constructor injection uses onConstructor_ instead of a hand-written constructor
Uses @Slf4j (or variant) instead of a manual Logger field
Does not import lombok.val or lombok.var

evals.prompt: Write two new Java classes in package com.example.util. First, a utility class StringUtils with a static method isBlank(String s) that returns true if the string is null or only whitespace. Second, a ValidationException that extends RuntimeException and supports the four standard constructors (no-arg, message, cause, message+cause). Use Lombok wherever possible — the project has Lombok on the classpath.
evals.expected_output: StringUtils annotated with @UtilityClass (no manual private constructor, no static keyword on methods). ValidationException annotated with @StandardException (no hand-written constructors).

assertions.text: Does not import lombok.val or lombok.var
